what_it_does: Bash CLI that installs, starts, stops, restarts, and reports status/health for five experimental Company TTS engine systemd --user services (chatterbox, orpheus, cosyvoice, xtts, qwen3tts) on fixed ports 4124-4128, plus a /health ping for the existing kokoro base on 4123 and an import check for the in-process ears stack (faster-whisper + silero-vad) in the loop venv.
observations: Uses systemd --user services, one per engine, with unit files symlinked from the repo into ~/.config/systemd/user.
Ports are hardcoded in an associative array: kokoro 4123, chatterbox 4124, orpheus 4125, cosyvoice 4126, xtts 4127, qwen3tts 4128.
The ears (faster-whisper + Silero VAD) are not systemd services; health only checks that the modules import inside $REPO/.voice-venv.
The install command symlinks unit files and runs daemon-reload but does not start services.
Status output includes the first GPU memory line from /usr/lib/wsl/lib/nvidia-smi, implying a WSL environment.
The ports command embeds usage notes: xtts is NON-COMMERCIAL, qwen3tts is TRIAL STARTER/no clip, chatterbox/cosyvoice/xtts need COMPANY_VOICE_REF, orpheus is heaviest VRAM.
Engines load models lazily on first run, as noted in the start command message.
An ENV_FILE path is shown in usage but the script never sources it.
standouts: Five competing TTS engines are orchestrated through a single bash wrapper around systemctl and curl.
Ears are treated as an in-process Python import dependency rather than a managed service.
GPU status is pulled from a hardcoded WSL nvidia-smi path.
The ports command doubles as a license/capability legend for the trial engines.
conventions: Follows bash strict mode with set -euo pipefail.
Uses functions (svc, units_for, cmd_install) and a case statement for subcommand dispatch.
Uses associative array for port lookup and a plain array for engine ordering.
Installs systemd units via symlinks rather than copies.
Hardcodes the repository root as /home/tim/company and the loop venv as $REPO/.voice-venv.
concerns: Absolute paths (/home/tim/company, /usr/lib/wsl/lib/nvidia-smi) make the script non-portable.
The ENV_FILE is advertised but never sourced, so environment requirements are unclear.
No validation that the requested engine exists before passing it to systemctl or journalctl.
enable-boot/disable-boot always apply to all engines, while start/stop/restart accept a single engine.
Health check only probes 127.0.0.1 and treats any non-200/timeout as down without detail.
install uses ln -sf without checking that source unit files actually exist.
notes: This is the operational control plane for the Company voice trial stack: a thin bash layer over systemd user services and curl health probes, with hardcoded ports and inline usage notes for each engine.
questions: What do the per-engine systemd unit files actually execute, and where are their per-engine venvs located?
How is COMPANY_VOICE_REF supplied to the engines that require it?
What is the exact relationship to ~/.local/bin/vllm-stack that this script is said to mirror?
How are the ears started and connected to the rest of the stack if they are only import-checked?
What does the /health endpoint return, and is a 200 response sufficient to confirm readiness?
How is kokoro managed if it is outside this script's scope?
purpose_vs_actual: Stated purpose matches actual behavior: it is a CLI wrapper for installing, controlling, and health-checking the voice trial engine services.
apparent_themes: Operational orchestration over systemd user services
Explicit trial/experimental stack with per-engine capability and licensing notes
Local development convenience with hardcoded environment assumptions
Service isolation via one systemd unit per engine
intention_signals: Provide a simple, discoverable control surface for a multi-engine voice synthesis trial stack so engineers can start, stop, inspect, and health-check all engines without remembering ports or unit names.
novelty: It bundles five distinct TTS engines under one bash CLI with a fixed port registry and inline commercial/trial annotations, and treats the ears stack as an in-process import dependency rather than a managed service.
contribution: A user-facing operational CLI that maps high-level commands to systemd user service units and health probes, and records a hardcoded registry of engine names, ports, and usage constraints for the trial stack.
summary_for_embedding: voice-stack is a bash CLI that installs, controls, and health-checks five experimental TTS engine systemd user services plus kokoro and an in-process ears import check for the Company voice trial stack.
intention_for_embedding: Intended to give engineers a single command to manage and verify the multi-engine voice trial stack running on fixed local ports.
